game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Fling!**: This grid-based puzzle game challenges players to clear a board of multiple "furballs" until only one remains. Players interact by swiping a single furball in one of four cardinal directions (up, down, left, right); the furball then slides until it hits another furball or the edge of the screen, pushing any furball it collides with in the same direction. Puzzles are solved by strategically pushing furballs off the screen, with each of the 30 difficulty levels introducing increasingly complex layouts and a higher number of furballs. The game features multiple modes, including Free-Play for untimed progression, Arcade mode where players race a global timer to solve as many puzzles as possible, and Challenge mode which requires solving sets of puzzles within specific time limits to unlock further levels. Hints and Undos are available, and the core loop involves analyzing the board, executing a sequence of swipes, and adapting to the unique solution of each puzzle.
game_info.description: Clear the board by strategically flinging furballs! Swipe a furball in any direction to send it sliding until it hits another furball or the edge. Push furballs off the screen to eliminate them. Your goal: leave only ONE furball remaining to complete each puzzle. Navigate through increasingly complex levels with more furballs and trickier layouts. Can you solve all the puzzles?
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Select which furball to fling (highlight cycles through available furballs)
WASD Keys: Fling the selected furball (W=Up, A=Left, S=Down, D=Right)
Z Key: Undo last move
Space: Skip to next level (after completing current level)
Enter: Start game / Continue after level complete
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic furball selection and flinging mechanics. This verifies that the player can select different furballs using arrow keys and fling them in all four directions using WASD keys. The test ensures collision detection works correctly when furballs hit each other or board edges.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The automated player cycles through furballs using RIGHT arrow key every 30 frames. Once a furball is selected, it randomly chooses one of the four WASD directions and flings the furball. This continues for 300 frames to test multiple fling sequences and collision scenarios.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Furballs should move smoothly when flung, stopping when hitting another furball or the board edge. Selected furball should be visually highlighted. Multiple furballs should be eliminated by pushing them off the board edges. The test is successful if no errors occur and furballs respond correctly to all directional inputs.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the complete win condition by solving the first level puzzle. This verifies that the game correctly detects when only one furball remains, transitions to the level complete state, and allows progression to the next level.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The automated player executes a pre-programmed sequence of moves designed to solve level 1. It selects specific furballs and flings them in predetermined directions to eliminate all but one furball. After completing the level, it presses SPACE to advance to the next level.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The level should be solved with only one furball remaining on the board. The game should display a "Level Complete" message and show the next level button. After pressing SPACE, the game should load level 2 with a new puzzle layout. Test succeeds if the win condition is properly detected and level progression works.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing the undo functionality to verify that players can reverse their moves. This ensures the game state is properly saved after each move and can be restored accurately, including furball positions and the move history.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The automated player makes several fling moves, then presses Z key to undo them one by one. It verifies that furballs return to their previous positions after each undo. The test performs 5 moves followed by 5 undos, then continues playing.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Each undo should restore the board to its exact state before the last move, including all furball positions. The move history counter should decrease with each undo. The test succeeds if all undos work correctly and the board state matches the expected configuration after each undo operation.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing edge cases and boundary conditions, specifically verifying that furballs pushed off the edge are properly removed from the game. This ensures the elimination mechanic works from all four sides of the board.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The automated player deliberately targets furballs near edges and flings them off in the direction of the nearest boundary. It attempts to push furballs off from all four sides (top, bottom, left, right) to ensure elimination works consistently from any edge.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Furballs pushed beyond the board boundaries should be immediately removed from the game and not counted toward the remaining furball total. The game should not crash or create visual artifacts when furballs are eliminated. Test passes if all edge eliminations work correctly.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing pause and resume functionality during active gameplay. This verifies that the game state is properly frozen when paused and resumes correctly without any state corruption or position drift.
TEST_5.strategy_description: The automated player plays normally for 60 frames, then presses ESC to pause. It waits 30 frames in paused state, then presses ESC again to resume. This sequence is repeated twice to ensure pause/resume works reliably multiple times in a single game session.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: When paused, all furball movement should stop and a pause overlay should appear. The game timer (if any) should also pause. Upon resume, gameplay should continue from the exact same state with no position changes or lost inputs. Test succeeds if pause/resume works flawlessly without state issues.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling by advancing through multiple levels. This verifies that each level loads with the correct number of furballs and layout, and that difficulty increases appropriately.
TEST_6.strategy_description: The automated player uses a combination of smart moves and random flinging to attempt solving multiple consecutive levels. After each level completion, it immediately presses SPACE to advance. The test continues for up to 5 levels or 1000 frames, whichever comes first.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Each new level should load with a fresh puzzle configuration. Later levels should have more furballs than earlier ones, demonstrating difficulty progression. Level numbers should increment correctly. Test passes if multiple levels can be loaded and played without errors or crashes.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing collision chain reactions where one furball pushes another, which in turn pushes a third furball. This verifies that the physics system correctly handles multi-furball interactions and sequential collisions.
TEST_7.strategy_description: The automated player looks for scenarios where three or more furballs are aligned in a row. It then flings the furball at one end to create a chain reaction. The test monitors whether all furballs in the chain move correctly and whether the final furball in the sequence is pushed off the edge if applicable.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: When a furball hits another, both should respond correctly according to physics rules. Chain reactions should propagate through multiple furballs smoothly. If the final furball reaches the edge, it should be eliminated. Test succeeds if all chain collision scenarios work without physics glitches or furballs passing through each other.
